v.ESV: Then Job answered the Lord and said:
v.KJV: Then Job answered the LORD, and said,
v.NASB: Then Job answered the LORD and said,
v.NIV: Then Job replied to the Lord :

v.CNV: “我知道你万事都能作，你的旨意是不能拦阻的。
v.ESV: “I know that you can do all things, and that no purpose of yours can be thwarted.
v.GAE: 주께서는 못 하실 일이 없사오며 무슨 계획이든지 못 이루실 것이 없는 줄 아오니
v.KJV: I know that thou canst do every thing, and that no thought can be withholden from thee.
v.NASB: 'I know that You can do all things, And that no purpose of Yours can be thwarted.
v.NIV: “I know that you can do all things; no purpose of yours can be thwarted.
v.NRSV: “I know that you can do all things, and that no purpose of yours can be thwarted.
v.SAENEW: 주님께서는 못하시는 일이 없으시다는 것을, 이제 저는 알았습니다. 주님의 계획은 어김없이 이루어진다는 것도, 저는 깨달았습니다.
v.WLB: “나는 주께서 모든 일을 하실 수 있고 계획하신 일은 무엇이든 이루신다는 것을 알았습니다.

v.CNV: 这以无知无识的言语使　神的旨意模糊不清的是谁呢？所以我说了我所不明白的；这些事太奇妙，是我不晓得的。
v.ESV: ‘Who is this that hides counsel without knowledge?’ Therefore I have uttered what I did not understand, things too wonderful for me, which I did not know.
v.GAE: 무지한 말로 이치를 가리는 자가 누구니이까 나는 깨닫지도 못한 일을 말하였고 스스로 알 수도 없고 헤아리기도 어려운 일을 말하였나이다
v.KJV: Who is he that hideth counsel without knowledge? therefore have I uttered that I understood not; things too wonderful for me, which I knew not.
v.NASB: 'Who is this that hides counsel without knowledge?' 'Therefore I have declared that which I did not understand, Things too wonderful for me, which I did not know.'
v.NIV: You asked, ‘Who is this that obscures my plans without knowledge?’ Surely I spoke of things I did not understand, things too wonderful for me to know.
v.NRSV: ‘Who is this that hides counsel without knowledge?’ Therefore I have uttered what I did not understand, things too wonderful for me, which I did not know.
v.SAENEW: 잘 알지도 못하면서, 감히 주님의 뜻을 흐려 놓으려 한 자가 바로 저입니다. 깨닫지도 못하면서, 함부로 말을 하였습니다. 제가 알기에는, 너무나 신기한 일들이었습니다.
v.WLB: 잘 알지도 못하고 주님의 뜻을 가린 자가 누구입니까? 내가 알지도 못하는 일들을 말하고 너무 기이해서 알 수 없는 일들을 내가 내뱉었습니다.

v.CNV: 求你听我，我要说话；我要问你，你要告诉我。
v.ESV: ‘Hear, and I will speak; I will question you, and you make it known to me.’
v.GAE: 내가 말하겠사오니 주는 들으시고 내가 주께 묻겠사오니 주여 내게 알게 하옵소서
v.KJV: Hear, I beseech thee, and I will speak: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.
v.NASB: 'Hear, now, and I will speak; I will ask You, and You instruct me.'
v.NIV: “You said, ‘Listen now, and I will speak; I will question you, and you shall answer me.’
v.NRSV: ‘Hear, and I will speak; I will question you, and you declare to me.’
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 말씀하셨습니다. "들어라. 내가 말하겠다. 내가 물을 터이니, 내게 대답하여라" 하셨습니다.
v.WLB: 간구하오니 들어주십시오. 내가 말하겠습니다. 내가 여쭙겠으니 대답해 주십시오.

v.CNV: 我从前只是风闻有你，但现在亲眼看见你。
v.ESV: I had heard of you by the hearing of the ear, but now my eye sees you;
v.GAE: 내가 주께 대하여 귀로 듣기만 하였사오나 이제는 눈으로 주를 뵈옵나이다
v.KJV: I have heard of thee by the hearing of the ear: but now mine eye seeth thee.
v.NASB: 'I have heard of You by the hearing of the ear; But now my eye sees You;
v.NIV: My ears had heard of you but now my eyes have seen you.
v.NRSV: I had heard of you by the hearing of the ear, but now my eye sees you;
v.SAENEW: 주님이 어떤 분이시라는 것을, 지금까지는 제가 귀로만 들었습니다. 그러나 이제는 제가 제 눈으로 주님을 뵙습니다.
v.WLB: 내가 주에 대해 지금까지 내 귀로만 들었는데 이제 내 눈으로 주를 보게 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 因此我厌恶自己，在尘土和灰烬中懊悔。”
v.ESV: therefore I despise myself, and repent in dust and ashes.”
v.GAE: 그러므로 내가 스스로 거두어들이고 티끌과 재 가운데에서 회개하나이다
v.KJV: Wherefore I abhor myself, and repent in dust and ashes.
v.NASB: Therefore I retract, And I repent in dust and ashes.'
v.NIV: Therefore I despise myself and repent in dust and ashes.”
v.NRSV: therefore I despise myself, and repent in dust and ashes.”
v.SAENEW: 그러므로 저는 제 주장을 거두어들이고, 티끌과 잿더미 위에 앉아서 회개합니다.
v.WLB: 그래서 내가 스스로 한탄하며 티끌과 재를 뒤집어쓰고 회개합니다.”

v.CNV: 耶和华对约伯说了这些话以后，就对提幔人以利法说：“我要向你和你的两个朋友生气，因为你们讲论我，不如我的仆人约伯说的对。
v.ESV: After the Lord had spoken these words to Job, the Lord said to Eliphaz the Temanite: “My anger burns against you and against your two friends, for you have not spoken of me what is right, as my servant Job has.
v.GAE: 여호와께서 욥에게 이 말씀을 하신 후에 여호와께서 데만 사람 엘리바스에게 이르시되 내가 너와 네 두 친구에게 노하나니 이는 너희가 나를 가리켜 말한 것이 내 종 욥의 말 같이 옳지 못함이니라
v.KJV: And it was so, that after the LORD had spoken these words unto Job, the LORD said to Eliphaz the Temanite, My wrath is kindled against thee, and against thy two friends: for ye have not spoken of me the thing that is right, as my servant Job hath.
v.NASB: It came about after the LORD had spoken these words to Job, that the LORD said to Eliphaz the Temanite, 'My wrath is kindled against you and against your two friends, because you have not spoken of Me what is right as My servant Job has.
v.NIV: After the Lord had said these things to Job , he said to Eliphaz the Temanite, “I am angry with you and your two friends, because you have not spoken the truth about me, as my servant Job has.
v.NRSV: After the LORD had spoken these words to Job, the LORD said to Eliphaz the Temanite: “My wrath is kindled against you and against your two friends; for you have not spoken of me what is right, as my servant Job has.
v.SAENEW: 주님께서는 욥에게 말씀을 마치신 다음에, 데만 사람 엘리바스에게 이렇게 말씀하셨다. "내가 너와 네 두 친구에게 분노한 것은, 너희가 나를 두고 말을 할 때에, 내 종 욥처럼 옳게 말하지 못하였기 때문이다.
v.WLB: 여호와께서는 욥에게 이 말씀을 마치시고 나서 데만 사람 엘리바스에게 말씀하셨습니다. “내가 너와 네 두 친구에게 진노했다. 너희는 나에 대해 내 종 욥처럼 옳게 말하지 않았다.

v.CNV: 现在你们要为自己取七头公牛、七只公羊，到我的仆人约伯那里去，为你们献上燔祭，我的仆人约伯就替你们祷告；我悦纳他的祷告，就不按照你们的愚昧待你们。你们讲论我，不如我的仆人约伯说的对。”
v.ESV: Now therefore take seven bulls and seven rams and go to my servant Job and offer up a burnt offering for yourselves. And my servant Job shall pray for you, for I will accept his prayer not to deal with you according to your folly. For you have not spoken of me what is right, as my servant Job has.”
v.GAE: 그런즉 너희는 수소 일곱과 숫양 일곱을 가지고 내 종 욥에게 가서 너희를 위하여 번제를 드리라 내 종 욥이 너희를 위하여 기도할 것인즉 내가 그를 기쁘게 받으리니 너희가 우매한 만큼 너희에게 갚지 아니하리라 이는 너희가 나를 가리켜 말한 것이 내 종 욥의 말 같이 옳지 못함이라
v.KJV: Therefore take unto you now seven bullocks and seven rams, and go to my servant Job, and offer up for yourselves a burnt offering; and my servant Job shall pray for you: for him will I accept: lest I deal with you after your folly, in that ye have not spoken of me the thing which is right, like my servant Job.
v.NASB: 'Now therefore, take for yourselves seven bulls and seven rams, and go to My servant Job, and offer up a burnt offering for yourselves, and My servant Job will pray for you. For I will accept him so that I may not do with you according to your folly, because you have not spoken of Me what is right, as My servant Job has.'
v.NIV: So now take seven bulls and seven rams and go to my servant Job and sacrifice a burnt offering for yourselves. My servant Job will pray for you, and I will accept his prayer and not deal with you according to your folly. You have not spoken the truth about me, as my servant Job has.”
v.NRSV: Now therefore take seven bulls and seven rams, and go to my servant Job, and offer up for yourselves a burnt offering; and my servant Job shall pray for you, for I will accept his prayer not to deal with you according to your folly; for you have not spoken of me what is right, as my servant Job has done.”
v.SAENEW: 그러므로 이제 너희는, 수송아지 일곱 마리와 숫양 일곱 마리를 마련하여, 내 종 욥에게 가지고 가서, 너희가 용서받을 수 있도록 번제를 드려라. 내 종 욥이 너희를 용서하여 달라고 빌면, 내가 그의 기도를 들어줄 것이다. 너희가 나를 두고 말을 할 때에, 내 종 욥처럼 옳게 말하지 않고, 어리석게 말하였지만, 내가 그대로 갚지는 않을 것이다."
v.WLB: 그러므로 너희는 수소 일곱 마리와 숫양 일곱 마리를 가지고 내 종 욥에게 가서 너희 자신을 위해 번제를 드려라. 내 종 욥이 너희를 위해 기도해 줄 것이다. 그러면 내가 그 기도를 받아들여 너희의 어리석음대로, 너희가 나에 대해 내 종 욥처럼 옳은 말을 하지 않은 것에 대해 갚지 않겠다.”

v.CNV: 于是提幔人以利法、书亚人比勒达、拿玛人琐法，照着耶和华吩咐他们的去行，耶和华就悦纳约伯的祷告。
v.ESV: So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went and did what the Lord had told them, and the Lord accepted Job's prayer.
v.GAE: 이에 데만 사람 엘리바스와 수아 사람 빌닷과 나아마 사람 소발이 가서 여호와께서 자기들에게 명령하신 대로 행하니라 여호와께서 욥을 기쁘게 받으셨더라
v.KJV: So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went, and did according as the LORD commanded them: the LORD also accepted Job.
v.NASB: So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went and did as the LORD told them; and the LORD accepted Job.
v.NIV: So Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite did what the Lord told them; and the Lord accepted Job’s prayer.
v.NRSV: So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went and did what the LORD had told them; and the LORD accepted Job’s prayer.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 데만 사람 엘리바스와 수아 사람 빌닷과 나아마 사람 소발이 가서, 주님께서 그들에게 말씀하신 대로 하니, 주님께서 욥의 기도를 들어주셨다.
v.WLB: 그러자 데만 사람 엘리바스와 수아 사람 빌닷과 나아마 사람 소발은 여호와의 말씀대로 했고 여호와께서는 욥의 기도를 받아들이셨습니다.

v.CNV: 约伯替他的朋友祷告，耶和华就恢复约伯原来的景况，还照约伯以前所有的一切加倍赐给他。
v.ESV: And the Lord restored the fortunes of Job, when he had prayed for his friends. And the Lord gave Job twice as much as he had before.
v.GAE: 욥이 그의 친구들을 위하여 기도할 때 여호와께서 욥의 곤경을 돌이키시고 여호와께서 욥에게 이전 모든 소유보다 갑절이나 주신지라
v.KJV: And the LORD turned the captivity of Job, when he prayed for his friends: also the LORD gave Job twice as much as he had before.
v.NASB: The LORD restored the fortunes of Job when he prayed for his friends, and the LORD increased all that Job had twofold.
v.NIV: After Job had prayed for his friends, the Lord restored his fortunes and gave him twice as much as he had before.
v.NRSV: And the LORD restored the fortunes of Job when he had prayed for his friends; and the LORD gave Job twice as much as he had before.
v.SAENEW: 욥이 주님께, 자기 친구들을 용서해 달라고 기도를 드리고 난 다음에, 주님께서 욥의 재산을 회복시켜 주셨는데, 욥이 이전에 가졌던 모든 것보다 배나 더 돌려주셨다.
v.WLB: 욥이 그 친구들을 위해 기도를 마치자 여호와께서는 욥의 상황을 돌이키셨고 전에 있었던 것보다 두 배로 더해 주셨습니다.

v.CNV: 约伯的众兄弟姊妹和以前认识的人都来见他，在他家里与他一同吃饭，又因耶和华降予他的一切灾祸都对他表同情，安慰他，每人送他一块银子和一个金环。
v.ESV: Then came to him all his brothers and sisters and all who had known him before, and ate bread with him in his house. And they showed him sympathy and comforted him for all the evil that the Lord had brought upon him. And each of them gave him a piece of money and a ring of gold.
v.GAE: 이에 그의 모든 형제와 자매와 이전에 알던 이들이 다 와서 그의 집에서 그와 함께 음식을 먹고 여호와께서 그에게 내리신 모든 재앙에 관하여 그를 위하여 슬퍼하며 위로하고 각각 케쉬타 하나씩과 금 고리 하나씩을 주었더라
v.KJV: Then came there unto him all his brethren, and all his sisters, and all they that had been of his acquaintance before, and did eat bread with him in his house: and they bemoaned him, and comforted him over all the evil that the LORD had brought upon him: every man also gave him a piece of money, and every one an earring of gold.
v.NASB: Then all his brothers and all his sisters and all who had known him before came to him, and they ate bread with him in his house; and they consoled him and comforted him for all the adversities that the LORD had brought on him. And each one gave him one piece of money, and each a ring of gold.
v.NIV: All his brothers and sisters and everyone who had known him before came and ate with him in his house. They comforted and consoled him over all the trouble the Lord had brought on him, and each one gave him a piece of silver and a gold ring.
v.NRSV: Then there came to him all his brothers and sisters and all who had known him before, and they ate bread with him in his house; they showed him sympathy and comforted him for all the evil that the LORD had brought upon him; and each of them gave him a piece of money and a gold ring.
v.SAENEW: 그러자 그의 모든 형제와 자매와 전부터 그를 아는 친구들이 다 그를 찾아와, 그의 집에서 그와 함께 기뻐하면서, 먹고 마셨다. 그들은 주님께서 그에게 내리신 그 모든 재앙을 생각하면서, 그를 동정하기도 하고, 또 위로하기도 하였다. 그러면서 그들은 저마다, 그에게 돈을 주기도 하고, 금반지를 끼워 주기도 하였다.
v.WLB: 그러자 그 모든 형제들과 자매들과 전에 알고 지냈던 사람들이 그에게 와서 그 집에서 함께 먹었습니다. 여호와께서 그에게 보내신 모든 고난을 위로하고 달래며 각각 은 한 조각과 금가락지를 욥에게 주었습니다.

v.CNV: 这样，耶和华后来赐给约伯的福比先前更多：他有一万四千只羊、六千匹骆驼、一千对牛、一千头母驴。
v.ESV: And the Lord blessed the latter days of Job more than his beginning. And he had 14,000 sheep, 6,000 camels, 1,000 yoke of oxen, and 1,000 female donkeys.
v.GAE: 여호와께서 욥의 말년에 욥에게 처음보다 더 복을 주시니 그가 양 만 사천과 낙타 육천과 소 천 겨리와 암나귀 천을 두었고
v.KJV: So the LORD blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning: for he had fourteen thousand sheep, and six thousand camels, and a thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand she asses.
v.NASB: The LORD blessed the latter days of Job more than his beginning; and he had 14,000 sheep and 6,000 camels and 1,000 yoke of oxen and 1,000 female donkeys.
v.NIV: The Lord blessed the latter part of Job’s life more than the former part. He had fourteen thousand sheep, six thousand camels, a thousand yoke of oxen and a thousand donkeys.
v.NRSV: The LORD blessed the latter days of Job more than his beginning; and he had fourteen thousand sheep, six thousand camels, a thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand donkeys.
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 욥의 말년에 이전보다 더 많은 복을 주셔서, 욥이, 양을 만 사천 마리, 낙타를 육천 마리, 소를 천 겨리, 나귀를 천 마리나 거느리게 하셨다.
v.WLB: 여호와께서는 욥의 말년에 초년보다 더 많은 복을 주셨습니다. 그리하여 그는 양 1만 4,000마리, 낙타 6,000마리, 소 1,000쌍, 암나귀 1,000마리를 갖게 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 他也有七个儿子和三个女儿。
v.ESV: He had also seven sons and three daughters.
v.GAE: 또 아들 일곱과 딸 셋을 두었으며
v.KJV: He had also seven sons and three daughters.
v.NASB: He had seven sons and three daughters.
v.NIV: And he also had seven sons and three daughters.
v.NRSV: He also had seven sons and three daughters.
v.SAENEW: 그리고 그는 아들 일곱과 딸 셋을 낳았다.
v.WLB: 그는 또 일곱 아들과 세 딸도 얻었습니다.

v.CNV: 他给长女起名叫耶米玛，次女名叫基洗亚，三女名叫基连．哈朴。
v.ESV: And he called the name of the first daughter Jemimah, and the name of the second Keziah, and the name of the third Keren-happuch.
v.GAE: 그가 첫째 딸은 여미마라 이름하였고 둘째 딸은 긋시아라 이름하였고 셋째 딸은 게렌합북이라 이름하였으니
v.KJV: And he called the name of the first, Jemima; and the name of the second, Kezia; and the name of the third, Keren-happuch.
v.NASB: He named the first Jemimah, and the second Keziah, and the third Keren-happuch.
v.NIV: The first daughter he named Jemimah, the second Keziah and the third Keren-Happuch.
v.NRSV: He named the first Jemimah, the second Keziah, and the third Keren-happuch.
v.SAENEW: 첫째 딸은 여미마, 둘째 딸은 긋시아, 셋째 딸은 게렌합북이라고 불렀다.
v.WLB: 그는 큰딸을 여미마, 둘째 딸을 긋시아, 셋째 딸을 게렌합북이라고 이름 지었습니다.

v.CNV: 在那全地中，找不到像约伯女儿那么美貌的妇女。她们的父亲在她们兄弟中间把产业分给她们。
v.ESV: And in all the land there were no women so beautiful as Job's daughters. And their father gave them an inheritance among their brothers.
v.GAE: 모든 땅에서 욥의 딸들처럼 아리따운 여자가 없었더라 그들의 아버지가 그들에게 그들의 오라비들처럼 기업을 주었더라
v.KJV: And in all the land were no women found so fair as the daughters of Job: and their father gave them inheritance among their brethren.
v.NASB: In all the land no women were found so fair as Job's daughters; and their father gave them inheritance among their brothers.
v.NIV: Nowhere in all the land were there found women as beautiful as Job’s daughters, and their father granted them an inheritance along with their brothers.
v.NRSV: In all the land there were no women so beautiful as Job’s daughters; and their father gave them an inheritance along with their brothers.
v.SAENEW: 땅 위의 어디에서도 욥의 딸들처럼 아리따운 여자를 찾아볼 수 없었다. 더욱이 그들의 아버지는, 오라비들에게 준 것과 똑같이, 딸들에게도 유산을 물려주었다.
v.WLB: 온 땅에서 욥의 딸들만큼 아름다운 여인들은 찾아볼 수 없었고 그 아버지는 아들들에게 준 것처럼 딸들에게도 유산을 나누어 주었습니다.

v.CNV: 此后，约伯又活了一百四十年，得见他的儿孙到四代。
v.ESV: And after this Job lived 140 years, and saw his sons, and his sons' sons, four generations.
v.GAE: 그 후에 욥이 백사십 년을 살며 아들과 손자 사 대를 보았고
v.KJV: After this lived Job an hundred and forty years, and saw his sons, and his sons’ sons, even four generations.
v.NASB: After this, Job lived 140 years, and saw his sons and his grandsons, four generations.
v.NIV: After this, Job lived a hundred and forty years; he saw his children and their children to the fourth generation.
v.NRSV: After this Job lived one hundred and forty years, and saw his children, and his children’s children, four generations.
v.SAENEW: 그 뒤에 욥은 백사십 년을 살면서, 그의 아들과 손자 사 대를 보았다.
v.WLB: 이후로 욥은 140년을 더 살면서 아들들과 손자들, 나아가 4대 자손까지 보았습니다.

v.CNV: 这样，约伯年纪老迈，寿终而死。
v.ESV: And Job died, an old man, and full of days.
v.KJV: So Job died, being old and full of days.
v.NASB: And Job died, an old man and full of days.
v.NIV: And so Job died, an old man and full of years.
v.NRSV: And Job died, old and full of days.
v.SAENEW: 욥은 이렇게 오래 살다가 세상을 떠났다.
v.WLB: 그러고 나서 욥은 나이 들어 수명이 다해 죽었습니다.
